project.creator: SonNT
project.notes: Feature-based workbook example. One feature sheet can contain multiple functions and multiple use cases.

changeLog.description: Reworked sample to group test cases by feature and function instead of by use case.

features.name: User Authentication
features.testRequirement: Validate register, login, and password recovery behavior across the authentication feature.

functions.name: Login
functions.description: Existing user signs in and starts an authenticated session.
functions.preCondition: Account exists and email is verified.

functions.name: Recover Password
functions.preCondition: Email already exists in the system.

testCases.procedure: 1. Open the sign-up page.\n2. Enter valid email, phone, and password.\n3. Submit the form.
testCases.expectedResults: 1. Account is created.\n2. OTP or verification step is triggered.
testCases.actualResults: Registration completed and verification flow started correctly.
testCases.dependencies: Email and phone number are not used by another account.

testCases.description: Reject registration when required fields are empty.
testCases.procedure: 1. Open the sign-up page.\n2. Leave required fields blank.\n3. Submit the form.
testCases.expectedResults: 1. Submission is blocked.\n2. Validation messages are displayed.
testCases.actualResults: Validation blocked submission, but the email error wording needs review.

testCases.procedure: 1. Open the login page.\n2. Observe the form fields and buttons.
testCases.expectedResults: 1. Email field is visible.\n2. Password field is visible.\n3. Login button is enabled.
testCases.actualResults: The login page loaded and the expected controls were visible.
testCases.dependencies: Application is reachable from the browser.

testCases.procedure: 1. Open the login page.\n2. Enter valid credentials.\n3. Click Login.
testCases.expectedResults: 1. Success message is shown.\n2. User reaches the dashboard.
testCases.actualResults: Login completed and the user was redirected to the dashboard.
testCases.dependencies: User account exists and is active.

testCases.description: Keep session when remember-me is enabled.
testCases.procedure:
1. Open the login page.
2. Enter valid credentials.
3. Enable remember me.
4. Login and reopen the browser.
testCases.expectedResults: 1. Session remains active across the configured persistence window.
testCases.dependencies: User account exists and browser settings allow cookies.

testCases.description: Reset password successfully with a valid OTP.
testCases.procedure:
1. Open forgot-password page.
2. Enter a registered email.
3. Receive OTP.
4. Verify OTP and set a new password.
testCases.expectedResults: 1. Password is updated successfully.\n2. User can return to the login page.
testCases.actualResults: Password reset completed and the user could log in with the new password.
testCases.dependencies: Email exists and OTP delivery works.
